dialogue_text: Then, I was stuck on a pole in a cornfield, where I could make-believe scare the crows, at any rate. But surely there is no use for a Scarecrow stuck on a pole in the middle of a river. I am afraid I shall never have any brains, after all!

dialogue_text: Something must be done to save us. I think I can swim to the shore and pull the raft after me, if you will only hold fast to the tip of my tail.
source_excerpt: "Something must be done to save us. I think I can swim to the shore and pull the raft after me, if you will only hold fast to the tip of my tail."

source_excerpt: "What shall we do now?" asked the Tin Woodman, as the Lion lay down on the grass to let the sun dry him.

dialogue_text: We must get back to the road, in some way,
source_excerpt: "We must get back to the road, in some way," said Dorothy.

dialogue_text: The best plan will be to walk along the riverbank until we come to the road again,
source_excerpt: "The best plan will be to walk along the riverbank until we come to the road again," remarked the Lion.

speaker.notes: No canonical speaker could be inferred from the source context.

source_excerpt: They walked along as fast as they could, Dorothy only stopping once to pick a beautiful flower; and after a time the Tin Woodman cried out: "Look!"
